message:
test(fcp-core): pin hex-decode error matrix (w674w)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/hex_decode_error_matrix.rs covering both
hex-decode surfaces:

  1. ObjectId::parse_prefixed (object.rs:39) — strips optional
     `objectid:` prefix exactly once, then hex::decodes.
  2. util::hex_or_bytes serde codec (util/hex_or_bytes.rs) — used by
     ObjectId, ZoneKeyId, NodeSignature.signature, etc. on JSON.

22 tests pin the documented contract:

- Empty input → WrongLength{actual:0} (NOT InvalidHex; the empty
  string is well-formed hex that decodes to 0 bytes).
- `objectid:` alone (post-strip empty) → WrongLength{actual:0}.
- Odd-length → InvalidHex (bare and prefixed).
- Non-hex char → InvalidHex (all-non-hex and single-char-corruption).
- Mixed-case hex (lowercase / uppercase / per-nibble alternating) all
  decode to the same bytes on both surfaces.
- `0x` prefix is NOT supported on either surface — only `objectid:`
  is documented as strippable. A `0x...` input MUST trip InvalidHex
  (parse_prefixed) or serde error (hex_or_bytes codec).
- `objectid:` prefix is stripped exactly ONCE; `objectid:objectid:...`
  fails because the second prefix reaches hex::decode literally.
- Wrong-length errors carry the actual decoded byte count.
- The two surfaces agree on the canonical 32-byte hex input.
- Display↔parse_prefixed round-trips via to_prefixed_string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
